text: admin-setupMonitoring [POST /admin.setupMonitoring]. Tags: admin. Update web server monitoring settings. Update the monitoring configuration for the web server including refresh rates, thresholds, and container services. Restarts the monitoring system and returns the updated settings. Disabled on cloud.. Parameters: metricsConfig (required)

application-create [POST /application.create]. Tags: application. Create an application. Creates a new application in the specified project environment. Supports GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, Git, Docker image, and drop sources.. Parameters: name (required), appName (optional) — unique internal service name (auto-generated, alphanumeric with dots/hyphens), description (optional), environmentId (required), serverId (optional)

application-one [GET /application.one]. Tags: application. Get an application. Retrieves detailed information about an application by its ID, including git provider access status and deployment configuration.. Parameters: applicationId (required)

application-reload [POST /application.reload]. Tags: application. Reload an application. Restarts the Docker container for the application by mechanizing it. Resets the application status to idle, then to done on success or error on failure.. Parameters: appName (required) — unique internal service name (auto-generated, alphanumeric with dots/hyphens), applicationId (required)

application-delete [POST /application.delete]. Tags: application. Delete an application. Permanently deletes an application and cleans up all associated resources including Docker services, Traefik configuration, deployments, middlewares, and source code.. Parameters: applicationId (required)

application-stop [POST /application.stop]. Tags: application. Stop an application. Stops the running Docker service for the application and sets its status to idle.. Parameters: applicationId (required)
application-start [POST /application.start]. Tags: application. Start an application. Starts the Docker service for the application and sets its status to done.. Parameters: applicationId (required)

application-redeploy [POST /application.redeploy]. Tags: application. Redeploy an application. Triggers a rebuild and redeployment of the application. Queues a deployment job or executes it directly for cloud servers.. Parameters: applicationId (required), title (optional), description (optional)

application-saveEnvironment [POST /application.saveEnvironment]. Tags: application. Save environment variables. Parameters: applicationId (required), env (required) — environment variables as a single string, one per line in KEY=VALUE format, buildArgs (required), buildSecrets (required), createEnvFile (required)

Parameters: applicationId (required), gitlabBranch (required), gitlabBuildPath (required), gitlabOwner (required), gitlabRepository (required), gitlabId (required), gitlabProjectId (required), gitlabPathNamespace (required), enableSubmodules (optional), watchPaths (optional) — array of file paths that trigger auto-deploy on change, e.g. ['src/**', 'package.json']

Parameters: bitbucketBranch (required), bitbucketBuildPath (required), bitbucketOwner (required), bitbucketRepository (required), bitbucketRepositorySlug (required), bitbucketId (required), applicationId (required), enableSubmodules (optional), watchPaths (optional) — array of file paths that trigger auto-deploy on change, e.g. ['src/**', 'package.json']

application-saveGiteaProvider [POST /application.saveGiteaProvider]. Tags: application. Save Gitea provider. Parameters: applicationId (required), giteaBranch (required), giteaBuildPath (required), giteaOwner (required), giteaRepository (required), giteaId (required), enableSubmodules (optional), watchPaths (optional) — array of file paths that trigger auto-deploy on change, e.g. ['src/**', 'package.json']

application-saveDockerProvider [POST /application.saveDockerProvider]. Tags: application. Save Docker provider. Configures the application to use a Docker image as its source, setting the image name, registry URL, and optional credentials.. Parameters: dockerImage (required) — Docker image name with optional tag, e.g. 'nginx:alpine' or 'postgres:16', applicationId (required), username (required), password (required), registryUrl (required)

application-saveGitProvider [POST /application.saveGitProvider]. Tags: application. Save Git provider. application-refreshToken [POST /application.refreshToken]. Tags: application. Refresh deploy token. Regenerates the webhook refresh token for the application, invalidating the previous token used for triggering deployments.. Parameters: applicationId (required)

application-deploy [POST /application.deploy]. Tags: application. Deploy an application. Triggers a new deployment for the application. Queues a deployment job or executes it directly for cloud servers.. Parameters: applicationId (required), title (optional), description (optional)

application-cleanQueues [POST /application.cleanQueues]. Tags: application. Clean deployment queues. Removes all pending deployment jobs from the queue for the specified application.. Parameters: applicationId (required)

application-clearDeployments [POST /application.clearDeployments]. Tags: application. Clear old deployments. Removes old deployment logs and artifacts for the application to free up disk space.. Parameters: applicationId (required)

application-killBuild [POST /application.killBuild]. Tags: application. Kill active build. Forcefully terminates the currently running Docker build process for the application.. Parameters: applicationId (required)

application-readTraefikConfig [GET /application.readTraefikConfig]. Tags: application. Read Traefik configuration. Reads the current Traefik reverse proxy configuration file for the application. Supports both local and remote server configurations.. Parameters: applicationId (required)

application-dropDeployment [POST /application.dropDeployment]. Tags: application. Deploy from zip upload. Deploys an application from an uploaded zip file. Unzips the file into the application directory and triggers a deployment.

application-updateTraefikConfig [POST /application.updateTraefikConfig]. Tags: application. Update Traefik configuration. Writes a new Traefik reverse proxy configuration for the application. Supports both local and remote server configurations.. Parameters: applicationId (required), traefikConfig (required)

application-readAppMonitoring [GET /application.readAppMonitoring]. Tags: application. Read application monitoring stats. Retrieves CPU and memory monitoring statistics for the application. Only available in self-hosted mode.. Parameters: appName (required)

application-move [POST /application.move]. Tags: application. Move application to another environment. Moves an application to a different environment within the same project or to another project's environment.. Parameters: applicationId (required), targetEnvironmentId (required)

application-cancelDeployment [POST /application.cancelDeployment]. Tags: application. Cancel a deployment. Cancels an in-progress deployment for the application and resets its status to idle. Only available in cloud version.. Parameters: applicationId (required)

application-search [GET /application.search]. Tags: application. Search applications. Searches applications by name, appName, description, repository, owner, or Docker image with pagination. Respects service-level access control.. Parameters: q (optional), name (optional), appName (optional), description (optional), repository (optional), owner (optional), dockerImage (optional), projectId (optional), environmentId (optional), limit (optional), offset (optional)

application-readLogs [GET /application.readLogs]. Tags: application. Read application logs. Retrieves Docker container logs for the application with configurable tail length, time range, and optional text search filtering.. Parameters: applicationId (required), tail (optional), since (optional), search (optional)

backup-create [POST /backup.create]. Tags: backup. Create a backup. Creates a new backup configuration for a database or compose service. If enabled, automatically schedules the backup according to the provided cron expression.. Parameters: schedule (required), enabled (optional), prefix (required), destinationId (required), keepLatestCount (optional), database (required), mariadbId (optional), mysqlId (optional), postgresId (optional), mongoId (optional), libsqlId (optional), databaseType (required) [postgres|mariadb|mysql|mongo|web-server|libsql], userId (optional), backupType (optional) [database|compose], composeId (optional), serviceName (optional), metadata (optional)
backup-one [GET /backup.one]. Tags: backup. Get a backup. Returns the details of a specific backup configuration by its ID.. Parameters: backupId (required)

backup-update [POST /backup.update]. Tags: backup. Update a backup. Updates an existing backup configuration. Reschedules or removes the backup job depending on the enabled state.. Parameters: schedule (required), enabled (required), prefix (required), backupId (required), destinationId (required), database (required), keepLatestCount (required), serviceName (required), metadata (required), databaseType (required) [postgres|mariadb|mysql|mongo|web-server|libsql]

backup-manualBackupMongo [POST /backup.manualBackupMongo]. Tags: backup. Run a MongoDB backup manually. Immediately executes a MongoDB backup using the specified backup configuration. Cleans up old backups according to retention settings.. Parameters: backupId (required)

backup-manualBackupLibsql [POST /backup.manualBackupLibsql]. Tags: backup. Run a LibSQL backup manually. Immediately executes a LibSQL backup using the specified backup configuration. Cleans up old backups according to retention settings.. Parameters: backupId (required)

backup-manualBackupWebServer [POST /backup.manualBackupWebServer]. Tags: backup. Run a web server backup manually. Immediately executes a web server backup using the specified backup configuration. Cleans up old backups according to retention settings.. Parameters: backupId (required)

backup-listBackupFiles [GET /backup.listBackupFiles]. Tags: backup. List backup files in S3. Lists backup files stored in the S3 destination bucket. Supports searching by path prefix and returns up to 100 results.. Parameters: destinationId (required), search (required), serverId (optional)

bitbucket-create [POST /bitbucket.create]. Tags: bitbucket. Create Bitbucket provider. Creates a new Bitbucket provider configuration linked to the active organization. Requires gitProviders create permission.. Parameters: bitbucketId (optional), bitbucketUsername (optional), bitbucketEmail (optional), appPassword (optional), apiToken (optional), bitbucketWorkspaceName (optional), gitProviderId (optional), authId (required), name (required)
bitbucket-one [GET /bitbucket.one]. Tags: bitbucket. Get Bitbucket provider. Returns a single Bitbucket provider configuration by its ID.. Parameters: bitbucketId (required)

bitbucket-bitbucketProviders [GET /bitbucket.bitbucketProviders]. Tags: bitbucket. List Bitbucket providers. Returns all Bitbucket providers accessible to the current user within the active organization.

bitbucket-getBitbucketBranches [GET /bitbucket.getBitbucketBranches]. Tags: bitbucket. List Bitbucket branches. Fetches the list of branches for a specific Bitbucket repository. Calls the Bitbucket API using the provider's credentials.. Parameters: owner (required), repo (required), bitbucketId (optional)

bitbucket-testConnection [POST /bitbucket.testConnection]. Tags: bitbucket. Test Bitbucket connection. Tests the connection to a Bitbucket provider by attempting to fetch its repositories. Returns the number of repositories found or throws an error on failure.. Parameters: bitbucketId (required), bitbucketUsername (optional), bitbucketEmail (optional), workspaceName (optional), apiToken (optional), appPassword (optional)

bitbucket-update [POST /bitbucket.update]. Tags: bitbucket. Update Bitbucket provider. Updates a Bitbucket provider configuration. Requires gitProviders create permission.. Parameters: bitbucketId (required), bitbucketUsername (optional), bitbucketEmail (optional), appPassword (optional), apiToken (optional), bitbucketWorkspaceName (optional), gitProviderId (required), name (required), organizationId (optional)

certificates-create [POST /certificates.create]. Tags: certificates. Create a certificate. Creates a new SSL/TLS certificate. In cloud mode, a server must be specified. Logs an audit entry upon creation.. Parameters: certificateId (optional), name (required), certificateData (required), privateKey (required), certificatePath (optional), autoRenew (optional), organizationId (required), serverId (optional)

certificates-one [GET /certificates.one]. Tags: certificates. Get a certificate. Returns a single certificate by its ID. Verifies that the certificate belongs to the current organization.. Parameters: certificateId (required)

certificates-remove [POST /certificates.remove]. Tags: certificates. Delete a certificate. Deletes a certificate by its ID after verifying organization ownership. Logs an audit entry before removal.. Parameters: certificateId (required)
certificates-all [GET /certificates.all]. Tags: certificates. List all certificates. Returns all certificates belonging to the current organization, including their associated server information.

certificates-update [POST /certificates.update]. Tags: certificates. Update a certificate. Updates the name, certificate data, and private key of an existing certificate. Verifies organization ownership before applying changes.. Parameters: certificateId (required), name (optional), certificateData (optional), privateKey (optional)
cluster-getNodes [GET /cluster.getNodes]. Tags: cluster. Get cluster nodes. Retrieves all nodes in the Docker Swarm cluster. Optionally targets a remote server.. Parameters: serverId (optional)

cluster-removeWorker [POST /cluster.removeWorker]. Tags: cluster. Remove a worker node. Drains and forcefully removes a worker node from the Docker Swarm cluster. An audit log entry is created for the removal.. Parameters: nodeId (required), serverId (optional)

cluster-addWorker [GET /cluster.addWorker]. Tags: cluster. Get worker join command. Returns the Docker Swarm join command and token for adding a new worker node to the cluster, along with the Docker version.. Parameters: serverId (optional)

compose-cleanQueues [POST /compose.cleanQueues]. Tags: compose. Clean deployment queues. Removes all pending deployment jobs from the queue for the specified compose service.. Parameters: composeId (required)

compose-clearDeployments [POST /compose.clearDeployments]. Tags: compose. Clear old deployments. Removes old deployment logs and artifacts for the compose service to free up disk space.. Parameters: composeId (required)

compose-killBuild [POST /compose.killBuild]. Tags: compose. Kill active build. Forcefully terminates the currently running Docker build process for the compose service.. Parameters: composeId (required)

compose-loadServices [GET /compose.loadServices]. Tags: compose. Load compose services. Parses the compose file and returns the list of services defined in it, with their current container status.. Parameters: composeId (required), type (optional)

compose-loadMountsByService [GET /compose.loadMountsByService]. Tags: compose. Load mounts by service. Retrieves the Docker volume mounts for a specific service within a compose stack by inspecting the running container.. Parameters: composeId (required), serviceName (required)

compose-fetchSourceType [POST /compose.fetchSourceType]. Tags: compose. Fetch and clone source. Clones the compose repository from the configured git provider and returns the source type. Executes the clone command locally or on a remote server.. Parameters: composeId (required)

compose-randomizeCompose [POST /compose.randomizeCompose]. Tags: compose. Randomize compose file. Parameters: composeId (required), suffix (optional) — custom suffix appended to the service name

compose-getConvertedCompose [GET /compose.getConvertedCompose]. Tags: compose. Get converted compose file. Returns the compose file with domains injected as Traefik labels, converted to YAML format ready for deployment.. Parameters: composeId (required)

compose-deploy [POST /compose.deploy]. Tags: compose. Deploy a compose service. Triggers a new deployment for the compose service. Queues a deployment job or executes it directly for cloud servers.. Parameters: composeId (required), title (optional), description (optional)

compose-refreshToken [POST /compose.refreshToken]. Tags: compose. Refresh deploy token. Regenerates the webhook refresh token for the compose service, invalidating the previous token used for triggering deployments.. Parameters: composeId (required)

compose-templates [GET /compose.templates]. Tags: compose. List available templates. Fetches the list of available compose templates from the GitHub templates repository.. Parameters: baseUrl (optional)
compose-getTags [GET /compose.getTags]. Tags: compose. Get template tags. Fetches all unique tags from the available compose templates for filtering purposes.. Parameters: baseUrl (optional)

compose-disconnectGitProvider [POST /compose.disconnectGitProvider]. Tags: compose. Disconnect git provider. compose-move [POST /compose.move]. Tags: compose. Move compose to another environment. Moves a compose service to a different environment within the same project or to another project's environment.. Parameters: composeId (required), targetEnvironmentId (required)

compose-cancelDeployment [POST /compose.cancelDeployment]. Tags: compose. Cancel a deployment. Cancels an in-progress deployment for the compose service and resets its status to idle. Only available in cloud version.. Parameters: composeId (required)

project-one [GET /project.one]. Tags: project. Get a project. Retrieves a project by its ID with all environments and services. Filters services based on the user's access permissions.. Parameters: projectId (required)

project-all [GET /project.all]. Tags: project. List all projects. Returns all projects in the current organization with their environments and services. Filters results based on the user's access permissions.

project-allForPermissions [GET /project.allForPermissions]. Tags: project. List all projects for permissions. Returns all projects with their environments and services for the permissions management UI. Requires member update permission.

project-search [GET /project.search]. Tags: project. Search projects. Searches projects by name or description with pagination. Respects project-level access control for non-admin users.. Parameters: q (optional), name (optional), description (optional), limit (optional), offset (optional)
project-remove [POST /project.remove]. Tags: project. Delete a project. Permanently deletes a project and all its associated environments, services, and resources.. Parameters: projectId (required)

project-update [POST /project.update]. Tags: project. Update a project. Updates a project's name, description, or environment variables. Validates organization ownership and project-level access permissions.. Parameters: projectId (required), name (optional), description (optional), createdAt (optional), organizationId (optional), env (optional) — environment variables as a single string, one per line in KEY=VALUE format

project-duplicate [POST /project.duplicate]. Tags: project. Duplicate a project or environment. Duplicates services from a source environment into a new project or into the same project. Copies applications, compose services, databases, and their associated domains, mounts, ports, and backups.. Parameters: sourceEnvironmentId (required), name (required), description (optional), includeServices (optional), selectedServices (optional), duplicateInSameProject (optional)

redirects-create [POST /redirects.create]. Tags: redirects. Create a redirect. Creates a new redirect rule for an application using a regex pattern. Logs an audit entry.. Parameters: regex (required), replacement (required), permanent (required), applicationId (required)
redirects-one [GET /redirects.one]. Tags: redirects. Get a redirect. Returns a single redirect rule by its ID.. Parameters: redirectId (required)
redirects-delete [POST /redirects.delete]. Tags: redirects. Delete a redirect. Deletes a redirect rule by its ID and logs an audit entry.. Parameters: redirectId (required)

redirects-update [POST /redirects.update]. Tags: redirects. Update a redirect. Updates an existing redirect rule's configuration and logs an audit entry.. Parameters: redirectId (required), regex (required), replacement (required), permanent (required)

redis-create [POST /redis.create]. Tags: redis. Create a Redis database. Creates a new Redis database service with the specified configuration, sets up a persistent data volume, and registers it in the project.. Parameters: name (required), appName (optional) — unique internal service name (auto-generated, alphanumeric with dots/hyphens), databasePassword (required) — password string for the database, dockerImage (optional) — Docker image name with optional tag, e.g. 'nginx:alpine' or 'postgres:16', environmentId (required), description (optional), serverId (optional)

redis-one [GET /redis.one]. Tags: redis. Get a Redis database by ID. Returns the full details of a Redis database service, including its environment and project configuration.. Parameters: redisId (required)
redis-start [POST /redis.start]. Tags: redis. Start a Redis database. Starts the Docker container for the specified Redis database and sets its status to done.. Parameters: redisId (required)

redis-reload [POST /redis.reload]. Tags: redis. Reload a Redis database. Restarts the Redis database by stopping and then starting its Docker container.. Parameters: redisId (required), appName (required) — unique internal service name (auto-generated, alphanumeric with dots/hyphens)
redis-stop [POST /redis.stop]. Tags: redis. Stop a Redis database. Stops the Docker container for the specified Redis database and sets its status to idle.. Parameters: redisId (required)

redis-saveExternalPort [POST /redis.saveExternalPort]. Tags: redis. Save the external port for a Redis database. Updates the external port mapping for the Redis database and triggers a redeployment. Validates that the port is not already in use.. Parameters: redisId (required), externalPort (required) — port number exposed to the host, e.g. 5432

redis-deploy [POST /redis.deploy]. Tags: redis. Deploy a Redis database. Triggers a deployment for the specified Redis database, rebuilding and restarting its Docker container with the current configuration.. Parameters: redisId (required)

redis-changeStatus [POST /redis.changeStatus]. Tags: redis. Change Redis database status. Updates the application status of a Redis database without starting or stopping the container.. Parameters: redisId (required), applicationStatus (required) [idle|running|done|error]
redis-remove [POST /redis.remove]. Tags: redis. Delete a Redis database. Removes the Redis database service, its Docker container, and deletes the database record.. Parameters: redisId (required)

redis-saveEnvironment [POST /redis.saveEnvironment]. Tags: redis. Save environment variables for a Redis database. Updates the environment variables for the specified Redis database service.. Parameters: redisId (required), env (required) — environment variables as a single string, one per line in KEY=VALUE format

redis-update [POST /redis.update]. Tags: redis. Update a Redis database. Updates the configuration of an existing Redis database service.. Parameters: redisId (required), name (optional), appName (optional) — unique internal service name (auto-generated, alphanumeric with dots/hyphens), description (optional), databasePassword (optional) — password string for the database, dockerImage (optional) — Docker image name with optional tag, e.g. 'nginx:alpine' or 'postgres:16', command (optional) — Docker CMD override, e.g. 'npm start' or 'python app.py', args (optional) — Docker command arguments as an array of strings, env (optional) — environment variables as a single string, one per line in KEY=VALUE format, memoryReservation (optional), memoryLimit (optional), cpuReservation (optional), cpuLimit (optional), externalPort (optional) — port number exposed to the host, e.g. 5432, createdAt (optional), applicationStatus (optional) [idle|running|done|error], healthCheckSwarm (optional), restartPolicySwarm (optional), placementSwarm (optional), updateConfigSwarm (optional), rollbackConfigSwarm (optional), modeSwarm (optional), labelsSwarm (optional), networkSwarm (optional), stopGracePeriodSwarm (optional), endpointSpecSwarm (optional), ulimitsSwarm (optional), replicas (optional), environmentId (optional)

redis-changePassword [POST /redis.changePassword]. Tags: redis. Change Redis database password. Changes the password for the Redis database by executing CONFIG SET requirepass inside the running container and updating the stored password.. Parameters: redisId (required), password (required)

redis-move [POST /redis.move]. Tags: redis. Move a Redis database to another environment. Moves the Redis database to a different environment within the same project.. Parameters: redisId (required), targetEnvironmentId (required)

redis-rebuild [POST /redis.rebuild]. Tags: redis. Rebuild a Redis database. Rebuilds the Redis database Docker container from scratch, pulling the latest image and recreating the service.. Parameters: redisId (required)

redis-search [GET /redis.search]. Tags: redis. Search Redis databases. Returns a paginated list of Redis databases matching the given filters. Supports searching by name, appName, description, project, and environment.. Parameters: q (optional), name (optional), appName (optional), description (optional), projectId (optional), environmentId (optional), limit (optional), offset (optional)

redis-readLogs [GET /redis.readLogs]. Tags: redis. Read Redis container logs. Retrieves the Docker container logs for the specified Redis database, with support for tail count, time-based filtering, and text search.. Parameters: redisId (required), tail (optional), since (optional), search (optional)

registry-create [POST /registry.create]. Tags: registry. Create registry. Creates a new Docker registry entry for the current organization and logs an audit event.. Parameters: registryName (required), username (required), password (required), registryUrl (required), registryType (required) [cloud], imagePrefix (required), serverId (optional)

registry-remove [POST /registry.remove]. Tags: registry. Delete registry. Removes a Docker registry entry by ID. Verifies organization ownership and logs an audit event before deletion.. Parameters: registryId (required)

registry-update [POST /registry.update]. Tags: registry. Update registry. Updates an existing Docker registry entry. Verifies organization ownership before applying changes and logs an audit event.. Parameters: registryId (required), registryName (optional), imagePrefix (optional), username (optional), password (optional), registryUrl (optional), createdAt (optional), registryType (optional) [cloud], organizationId (optional), serverId (optional)
registry-all [GET /registry.all]. Tags: registry. List all registries. Returns all Docker registry entries for the current organization.
registry-one [GET /registry.one]. Tags: registry. Get registry. Returns a single Docker registry entry by ID. Verifies the caller belongs to the same organization.. Parameters: registryId (required)

registry-testRegistry [POST /registry.testRegistry]. Tags: registry. Test registry credentials. Attempts a docker login with the provided credentials to verify the registry URL, username, and password are valid. Can run locally or on a remote server.. Parameters: registryName (optional), username (required), password (required), registryUrl (required), registryType (required) [cloud], imagePrefix (optional), serverId (optional)

registry-testRegistryById [POST /registry.testRegistryById]. Tags: registry. Test registry connection by ID. Looks up a saved registry by ID and attempts a docker login with its stored credentials. Verifies organization ownership before testing.. Parameters: registryId (optional), serverId (optional)

security-create [POST /security.create]. Tags: security. Create a security entry. Creates a new HTTP basic auth security entry for an application with the provided username and password. Logs an audit entry.. Parameters: applicationId (required), username (required), password (required)
security-one [GET /security.one]. Tags: security. Get a security entry. Returns a single HTTP basic auth security entry by its ID.. Parameters: securityId (required)
security-delete [POST /security.delete]. Tags: security. Delete a security entry. Deletes an HTTP basic auth security entry by its ID and logs an audit entry.. Parameters: securityId (required)

security-update [POST /security.update]. Tags: security. Update a security entry. Updates an existing HTTP basic auth security entry's configuration and logs an audit entry.. Parameters: securityId (required), username (required), password (required)

server-create [POST /server.create]. Tags: server. Create a server. Creates a new server in the organization. In cloud mode, enforces the user's server quantity limit. Returns the newly created server.. Parameters: name (required), description (required), ipAddress (required), port (required), username (required), sshKeyId (required), serverType (required) [deploy|build]

server-one [GET /server.one]. Tags: server. Get a server. Retrieves a single server by its ID. Validates that the user has access to the server within their organization.. Parameters: serverId (required)

server-getDefaultCommand [GET /server.getDefaultCommand]. Tags: server. Get default server command. Returns the default setup command for a server. The command varies depending on whether the server is a build server or a deploy server.. Parameters: serverId (required)

server-all [GET /server.all]. Tags: server. List all servers. Returns all servers in the current organization along with a count of associated services (applications, compose, databases). Results are filtered by the user's accessible server permissions.

server-allForPermissions [GET /server.allForPermissions]. Tags: server. List all servers for permissions. Returns a minimal list of servers (ID, name, IP, type) used for configuring member permissions. Requires a valid enterprise license.
server-count [GET /server.count]. Tags: server. Get server count. Returns the total number of servers across all organizations owned by the current user.

server-withSSHKey [GET /server.withSSHKey]. Tags: server. List servers with SSH keys. Returns all deploy-type servers that have an SSH key configured. In cloud mode, only active servers are included. Results are filtered by the user's accessible server permissions.

server-buildServers [GET /server.buildServers]. Tags: server. List build servers. Returns all build-type servers that have an SSH key configured. In cloud mode, only active servers are included. Results are filtered by the user's accessible server permissions.

server-setup [POST /server.setup]. Tags: server. Setup a server. Runs the initial setup process on a remote server, installing required dependencies and configuring Docker. An audit log entry is created.. Parameters: serverId (required)

server-validate [GET /server.validate]. Tags: server. Validate server configuration. Checks the server for required tools and configuration including Docker, Rclone, Nixpacks, Buildpacks, Railpack, Swarm mode, network setup, and privilege mode.. Parameters: serverId (required)

server-security [GET /server.security]. Tags: server. Get server security audit. Performs a security audit on the server, checking UFW firewall, SSH configuration, non-root user setup, unattended upgrades, and Fail2Ban status.. Parameters: serverId (required)

server-setupMonitoring [POST /server.setupMonitoring]. Tags: server. Setup server monitoring. Configures and deploys the monitoring agent on a server with the specified metrics configuration including refresh rates, retention, thresholds, and container service filters.. Parameters: serverId (required), metricsConfig (required)

server-remove [POST /server.remove]. Tags: server. Remove a server. Deletes a server and removes all associated deployments. Fails if the server has active services. In cloud mode, updates the user's server quantity allocation.. Parameters: serverId (required)

server-update [POST /server.update]. Tags: server. Update a server. Updates the configuration of an existing server. Fails if the server is inactive. An audit log entry is created for the update.. Parameters: name (required), description (required), serverId (required), ipAddress (required), port (required), username (required), sshKeyId (required), serverType (required) [deploy|build], command (optional) — Docker CMD override, e.g. 'npm start' or 'python app.py'
server-publicIp [GET /server.publicIp]. Tags: server. Get public IP address. Returns the public IP address of the local server. Returns an empty string in cloud mode.
server-getServerTime [GET /server.getServerTime]. Tags: server. Get server time. Returns the current server time and timezone. Returns null in cloud mode.

server-getServerMetrics [GET /server.getServerMetrics]. Tags: server. Get server metrics. Fetches monitoring metrics (CPU, memory, disk, network) from the server's monitoring agent endpoint. Requires the monitoring service to be configured and running.. Parameters: url (required), token (required), dataPoints (required)
settings-getWebServerSettings [GET /settings.getWebServerSettings]. Tags: settings. Get web server settings. Retrieve the current web server settings. Returns null on cloud.
settings-reloadServer [POST /settings.reloadServer]. Tags: settings. Reload Dokploy server. Reload the Dokploy Docker service with the current version. Disabled on cloud.
settings-cleanRedis [POST /settings.cleanRedis]. Tags: settings. Flush Redis data. Execute FLUSHALL on the Dokploy Redis container, removing all cached data. Disabled on cloud.
settings-reloadRedis [POST /settings.reloadRedis]. Tags: settings. Reload Redis service. Force-reload the Dokploy Redis Docker service. Disabled on cloud.
settings-cleanAllDeploymentQueue [POST /settings.cleanAllDeploymentQueue]. Tags: settings. Clean all deployment queues. Remove all pending jobs from the deployment queue. Disabled on cloud.

settings-reloadTraefik [POST /settings.reloadTraefik]. Tags: settings. Reload Traefik service. Force-reload the Dokploy Traefik Docker service. Runs in the background to avoid proxy timeouts. Optionally targets a specific server.. Parameters: serverId (optional)

settings-toggleDashboard [POST /settings.toggleDashboard]. Tags: settings. Toggle Traefik dashboard. Enable or disable the Traefik dashboard by adding or removing port 8080. Checks for port conflicts before enabling. Runs the Traefik setup in the background.. Parameters: enableDashboard (optional), serverId (optional)

settings-cleanUnusedImages [POST /settings.cleanUnusedImages]. Tags: settings. Clean unused Docker images. Remove all unused Docker images from the host or a specified server.. Parameters: serverId (optional)

settings-cleanUnusedVolumes [POST /settings.cleanUnusedVolumes]. Tags: settings. Clean unused Docker volumes. Remove all unused Docker volumes from the host or a specified server.. Parameters: serverId (optional)

settings-cleanStoppedContainers [POST /settings.cleanStoppedContainers]. Tags: settings. Clean stopped Docker containers. Remove all stopped Docker containers from the host or a specified server.. Parameters: serverId (optional)

settings-cleanDockerBuilder [POST /settings.cleanDockerBuilder]. Tags: settings. Clean Docker build cache. Remove Docker builder cache from the host or a specified server.. Parameters: serverId (optional)

settings-cleanDockerPrune [POST /settings.cleanDockerPrune]. Tags: settings. Prune Docker system. Run a full Docker system prune and builder cache cleanup on the host or a specified server.. Parameters: serverId (optional)

settings-cleanAll [POST /settings.cleanAll]. Tags: settings. Clean all Docker resources. Run a comprehensive Docker cleanup (images, containers, volumes, builders, system prune) in the background to avoid gateway timeouts.. Parameters: serverId (optional)
settings-cleanMonitoring [POST /settings.cleanMonitoring]. Tags: settings. Clean monitoring data. Delete and recreate the monitoring data directory, removing all collected metrics. Disabled on cloud.
settings-getDockerDiskUsage [GET /settings.getDockerDiskUsage]. Tags: settings. Get Docker disk usage. Retrieve Docker disk usage statistics. Returns an empty array on cloud.

settings-saveSSHPrivateKey [POST /settings.saveSSHPrivateKey]. Tags: settings. Save SSH private key. Store an SSH private key in the web server settings for remote server access. Disabled on cloud.. Parameters: sshPrivateKey (required)

settings-assignDomainServer [POST /settings.assignDomainServer]. Tags: settings. Assign domain to server. Configure the server domain, HTTPS settings, certificate type, and Let's Encrypt email. Updates both web server settings and Traefik configuration. Disabled on cloud.. Parameters: host (required) — domain hostname, e.g. 'myapp.example.com', certificateType (required) [letsencrypt|none|custom], letsEncryptEmail (optional), https (optional)
settings-cleanSSHPrivateKey [POST /settings.cleanSSHPrivateKey]. Tags: settings. Remove SSH private key. Clear the stored SSH private key from web server settings. Disabled on cloud.

settings-updateDockerCleanup [POST /settings.updateDockerCleanup]. Tags: settings. Update Docker cleanup schedule. Enable or disable automatic Docker cleanup for a server or the web server. When enabled, schedules a cron job that periodically removes unused Docker resources and sends notifications.. Parameters: enableDockerCleanup (required), serverId (optional)
settings-readTraefikConfig [GET /settings.readTraefikConfig]. Tags: settings. Read main Traefik configuration. Read the main Traefik configuration file. Disabled on cloud.

settings-updateTraefikConfig [POST /settings.updateTraefikConfig]. Tags: settings. Update main Traefik configuration. Overwrite the main Traefik configuration file with the provided content. Disabled on cloud.. Parameters: traefikConfig (required)

settings-readWebServerTraefikConfig [GET /settings.readWebServerTraefikConfig]. Tags: settings. Read web server Traefik configuration. Read the Dokploy-specific Traefik configuration file. Disabled on cloud.

settings-updateWebServerTraefikConfig [POST /settings.updateWebServerTraefikConfig]. Tags: settings. Update web server Traefik configuration. Overwrite the Dokploy-specific Traefik configuration file with the provided content. Disabled on cloud.. Parameters: traefikConfig (required)

settings-readMiddlewareTraefikConfig [GET /settings.readMiddlewareTraefikConfig]. Tags: settings. Read middleware Traefik configuration. Read the Traefik middlewares configuration file. Disabled on cloud.

settings-updateMiddlewareTraefikConfig [POST /settings.updateMiddlewareTraefikConfig]. Tags: settings. Update middleware Traefik configuration. Overwrite the Traefik middlewares configuration file with the provided content. Disabled on cloud.. Parameters: traefikConfig (required)
settings-getUpdateData [POST /settings.getUpdateData]. Tags: settings. Check for Dokploy updates. Check whether a newer version of Dokploy is available. Returns default data on cloud.

settings-updateServer [POST /settings.updateServer]. Tags: settings. Update Dokploy to latest version. Pull the latest Dokploy Docker image and update the service if a new version is available. Disabled on cloud.
settings-getDokployVersion [GET /settings.getDokployVersion]. Tags: settings. Get Dokploy version. Return the currently running Dokploy version from package.json.
settings-getReleaseTag [GET /settings.getReleaseTag]. Tags: settings. Get Dokploy release tag. Return the Docker image tag of the running Dokploy instance.

settings-readDirectories [GET /settings.readDirectories]. Tags: settings. List Traefik configuration directories. Read the directory listing of the main Traefik configuration path. Requires traefikFiles.read permission.. Parameters: serverId (optional)

settings-updateTraefikFile [POST /settings.updateTraefikFile]. Tags: settings. Update a Traefik configuration file. Write content to a specific Traefik configuration file at the given path. Requires traefikFiles.write permission.. Parameters: path (required), traefikConfig (required), serverId (optional)

settings-readTraefikFile [GET /settings.readTraefikFile]. Tags: settings. Read a Traefik configuration file. Read the content of a specific Traefik configuration file at the given path. Requires traefikFiles.read permission. Validates server ownership when a serverId is provided.. Parameters: path (required), serverId (optional)
settings-getIp [GET /settings.getIp]. Tags: settings. Get server IP address. Return the configured server IP address from web server settings. Returns an empty string on cloud.

settings-updateServerIp [POST /settings.updateServerIp]. Tags: settings. Update server IP address. Update the server IP address stored in web server settings. Disabled on cloud.. Parameters: serverIp (required)

settings-getOpenApiDocument [GET /settings.getOpenApiDocument]. Tags: settings. Get OpenAPI specification. Generate and return the full OpenAPI document for the Dokploy API, including all endpoints, tags, and security schemes.

settings-readTraefikEnv [GET /settings.readTraefikEnv]. Tags: settings. Read Traefik environment variables. Retrieve the environment variables configured for the Dokploy Traefik service, optionally for a specific server.. Parameters: serverId (optional)

settings-writeTraefikEnv [POST /settings.writeTraefikEnv]. Tags: settings. Write Traefik environment variables. Update the environment variables for the Dokploy Traefik service and restart it in the background. Preserves the current port configuration.. Parameters: env (required) — environment variables as a single string, one per line in KEY=VALUE format, serverId (optional)

settings-haveTraefikDashboardPortEnabled [GET /settings.haveTraefikDashboardPortEnabled]. Tags: settings. Check Traefik dashboard port status. Check whether port 8080 (Traefik dashboard) is currently enabled in the Traefik service port configuration.. Parameters: serverId (optional)

settings-haveActivateRequests [GET /settings.haveActivateRequests]. Tags: settings. Check if request logging is active. Check whether Traefik access log (request logging) is enabled by inspecting the main Traefik configuration. Returns true on cloud.

settings-toggleRequests [POST /settings.toggleRequests]. Tags: settings. Toggle request logging. Enable or disable Traefik access log (request logging) by updating the main Traefik configuration file. Disabled on cloud.. Parameters: enable (required)
settings-isCloud [GET /settings.isCloud]. Tags: settings. Check if running on cloud. Return whether this Dokploy instance is running in cloud mode.
settings-isUserSubscribed [GET /settings.isUserSubscribed]. Tags: settings. Check if user has resources. Check whether the current organization has any servers or projects, indicating active usage.
settings-health [GET /settings.health]. Tags: settings. Health check. Verify the application is running and the database connection is healthy by executing a simple query.

settings-checkInfrastructureHealth [GET /settings.checkInfrastructureHealth]. Tags: settings. Check infrastructure health. Check the health status of PostgreSQL, Redis, and Traefik services. Returns healthy for all on cloud.

settings-setupGPU [POST /settings.setupGPU]. Tags: settings. Set up GPU support. Install and configure GPU support (NVIDIA runtime) on the host or a specified server. On cloud, a serverId is required.. Parameters: serverId (optional)

settings-checkGPUStatus [GET /settings.checkGPUStatus]. Tags: settings. Check GPU status. Retrieve detailed GPU status including driver version, CUDA support, memory info, available GPUs, and Docker Swarm GPU resources for the host or a specified server.. Parameters: serverId (optional)

settings-updateTraefikPorts [POST /settings.updateTraefikPorts]. Tags: settings. Update Traefik ports. Replace the Traefik service port mappings with the provided list. Checks for port conflicts before applying. Runs the Traefik setup in the background.. Parameters: serverId (optional), additionalPorts (required)

settings-getTraefikPorts [GET /settings.getTraefikPorts]. Tags: settings. Get Traefik ports. Retrieve the current port mappings configured for the Dokploy Traefik service.. Parameters: serverId (optional)

settings-updateLogCleanup [POST /settings.updateLogCleanup]. Tags: settings. Update log cleanup schedule. Start or stop the automatic log cleanup cron job. Provide a cron expression to start, or null to stop. Disabled on cloud.. Parameters: cronExpression (required)
settings-getLogCleanupStatus [GET /settings.getLogCleanupStatus]. Tags: settings. Get log cleanup status. Return the current status and schedule of the automatic log cleanup job.

settings-getDokployCloudIps [GET /settings.getDokployCloudIps]. Tags: settings. Get Dokploy cloud IP addresses. Return the list of Dokploy cloud IP addresses from the environment configuration. Returns an empty array in self-hosted mode.

sshKey-create [POST /sshKey.create]. Tags: sshKey. Create SSH key. Stores a new SSH key for the current organization and logs an audit event.. Parameters: name (required), description (optional), privateKey (required), publicKey (required), organizationId (required)
sshKey-remove [POST /sshKey.remove]. Tags: sshKey. Delete SSH key. Removes an SSH key by ID. Verifies organization ownership and logs an audit event before deletion.. Parameters: sshKeyId (required)
sshKey-one [GET /sshKey.one]. Tags: sshKey. Get SSH key. Returns a single SSH key by ID. Verifies the caller belongs to the same organization.. Parameters: sshKeyId (required)
sshKey-all [GET /sshKey.all]. Tags: sshKey. List all SSH keys. Returns all SSH keys for the current organization, ordered by creation date descending.

sshKey-allForApps [GET /sshKey.allForApps]. Tags: sshKey. List SSH keys for app selection. Returns a lightweight list of SSH keys (ID and name only) for the current organization, suitable for dropdown selectors in application forms.

sshKey-generate [POST /sshKey.generate]. Tags: sshKey. Generate SSH key pair. Generates a new SSH key pair of the specified type (RSA, ED25519, etc.) and returns both public and private keys.. Parameters: type (optional) [rsa|ed25519]

sshKey-update [POST /sshKey.update]. Tags: sshKey. Update SSH key. Updates an existing SSH key. Verifies organization ownership before applying changes and logs an audit event.. Parameters: name (optional), description (optional), lastUsedAt (optional), sshKeyId (required)

stripe-getCurrentPlan [GET /stripe.getCurrentPlan]. Tags: stripe. Get current billing plan. Returns the active Stripe billing plan (hobby, startup, or legacy) for the caller's organization owner. Returns null if not on cloud or no subscription exists.

stripe-getProducts [GET /stripe.getProducts]. Tags: stripe. List Stripe products and subscriptions. Returns available Stripe products, the user's active subscriptions, current plan tier, billing interval, and price amount.

stripe-createCheckoutSession [POST /stripe.createCheckoutSession]. Tags: stripe. Create Stripe checkout session. Creates a Stripe checkout session for subscribing to a billing plan. Handles customer creation or reuse and returns the session ID for redirect.. Parameters: tier (required) [legacy|hobby|startup], productId (required), serverQuantity (required), isAnnual (required)

stripe-createCustomerPortalSession [POST /stripe.createCustomerPortalSession]. Tags: stripe. Create Stripe customer portal session. Creates a Stripe billing portal session URL so the user can manage their subscription, payment methods, and invoices.

stripe-upgradeSubscription [POST /stripe.upgradeSubscription]. Tags: stripe. Upgrade subscription. Upgrades or changes the current Stripe subscription to a different tier or server quantity. Applies prorated charges for the billing period change.. Parameters: tier (required) [hobby|startup], serverQuantity (required), isAnnual (required)

stripe-canCreateMoreServers [GET /stripe.canCreateMoreServers]. Tags: stripe. Check server creation quota. Returns whether the organization can create more servers based on their subscription's server quantity limit. Always returns true for self-hosted instances.

stripe-updateInvoiceNotifications [POST /stripe.updateInvoiceNotifications]. Tags: stripe. Update invoice notification preference. Enables or disables email notifications for invoice events. Only available on Dokploy Cloud.. Parameters: enabled (required)
stripe-getInvoices [GET /stripe.getInvoices]. Tags: stripe. List invoices. Returns up to 100 Stripe invoices for the organization owner, including status, amounts, and download links.
swarm-getNodes [GET /swarm.getNodes]. Tags: swarm. Get Swarm nodes. Retrieves all nodes in the Docker Swarm. Optionally targets a remote server by ID.. Parameters: serverId (optional)

swarm-getNodeInfo [GET /swarm.getNodeInfo]. Tags: swarm. Get Swarm node info. Retrieves detailed information about a specific Docker Swarm node by its node ID. Optionally targets a remote server.. Parameters: nodeId (required), serverId (optional)

swarm-getNodeApps [GET /swarm.getNodeApps]. Tags: swarm. Get Swarm node applications. Retrieves all applications (services) running across Docker Swarm nodes. Optionally targets a remote server.. Parameters: serverId (optional)

swarm-getContainerStats [GET /swarm.getContainerStats]. Tags: swarm. Get container stats. Retrieves resource usage statistics for all containers. Optionally targets a remote server and validates organization access.. Parameters: serverId (optional)
user-all [GET /user.all]. Tags: user. List all organization members. Retrieve all members of the current active organization, including their associated user data, ordered by creation date.

user-one [GET /user.one]. Tags: user. Get a user by ID. Retrieve a specific user's membership and profile within the active organization. Users can view their own data; admins and owners can view any member. Requires member.update permission for non-self lookups.. Parameters: userId (required)
user-session [GET /user.session]. Tags: user. Get current session. Return the current user's ID and active organization ID from the session. Returns null if no valid session exists.
user-get [GET /user.get]. Tags: user. Get current user profile. Retrieve the current authenticated user's membership record including user profile and API keys for the active organization.

user-getPermissions [GET /user.getPermissions]. Tags: user. Get resolved permissions. Return the fully resolved permissions for the current user in the active organization, combining role-based and custom permissions.

user-haveRootAccess [GET /user.haveRootAccess]. Tags: user. Check root access. Check whether the current user has root admin access. Only returns true in cloud mode for the designated admin user or impersonating sessions.
user-getBackups [GET /user.getBackups]. Tags: user. Get user backups. Retrieve the current admin user's backup configurations including destinations, deployments, and API keys.
user-getServerMetrics [GET /user.getServerMetrics]. Tags: user. Get server metrics user. Retrieve the user record associated with server metrics access for the current organization membership.

user-update [POST /user.update]. Tags: user. Update current user. Update the current user's profile. If changing the password, the current password must be provided and verified. Parameters: id (optional), firstName (optional), lastName (optional), isRegistered (optional), expirationDate (optional), createdAt2 (optional), createdAt (optional), twoFactorEnabled (optional), email (optional), emailVerified (optional), image (optional), banned (optional), banReason (optional), banExpires (optional), updatedAt (optional), enablePaidFeatures (optional), allowImpersonation (optional), enableEnterpriseFeatures (optional), licenseKey (optional), stripeCustomerId (optional), stripeSubscriptionId (optional), serversQuantity (optional), sendInvoiceNotifications (optional), password (optional), currentPassword (optional)

user-getMetricsToken [GET /user.getMetricsToken]. Tags: user. Get metrics token and configuration. Retrieve the server IP, paid features flag, and monitoring configuration needed for metrics collection.

user-remove [POST /user.remove]. Tags: user. Remove a user. Delete a user from the organization. Only owners and admins can remove users; owners cannot be removed, and admins cannot remove themselves or other admins. Disabled on cloud.. Parameters: userId (required)

user-assignPermissions [POST /user.assignPermissions]. Tags: user. Assign member permissions. Update permissions for a specific member in the organization. Only the organization owner can assign permissions. Git provider and server access restrictions require a valid license.. Parameters: id (required), accessedProjects (required), accessedEnvironments (required), accessedServices (required), accessedGitProviders (required), accessedServers (required), canCreateProjects (required), canCreateServices (required), canDeleteProjects (required), canDeleteServices (required), canAccessToDocker (required), canAccessToTraefikFiles (required), canAccessToAPI (required), canAccessToSSHKeys (required), canAccessToGitProviders (required), canDeleteEnvironments (required), canCreateEnvironments (required)

user-getInvitations [GET /user.getInvitations]. Tags: user. Get pending invitations for current user. Retrieve all pending organization invitations for the current user's email that have not yet expired.

user-getContainerMetrics [GET /user.getContainerMetrics]. Tags: user. Get container metrics. Fetch monitoring metrics for a specific container by querying the metrics endpoint. Requires an application name, metrics URL, and authentication token.. Parameters: url (required), token (required), appName (required), dataPoints (required)
user-generateToken [POST /user.generateToken]. Tags: user. Generate authentication token. Generate a new authentication token for the current user.

user-deleteApiKey [POST /user.deleteApiKey]. Tags: user. Delete an API key. Delete an API key by ID. Only the owner of the API key can delete it. Logs an audit event on success.. Parameters: apiKeyId (required)

user-createApiKey [POST /user.createApiKey]. Tags: user. Create an API key. Create a new API key for the current user, scoped to a specific organization. Supports optional rate limiting and request limiting configuration.. Parameters: name (required), prefix (optional), expiresIn (optional), metadata (required), rateLimitEnabled (optional), rateLimitTimeWindow (optional), rateLimitMax (optional), remaining (optional), refillAmount (optional), refillInterval (optional)

user-checkUserOrganizations [GET /user.checkUserOrganizations]. Tags: user. Check user organization count. Return the number of organizations a user belongs to. Users can check their own count; admins and owners can check counts for members in the active organization.. Parameters: userId (required)

user-createUserWithCredentials [POST /user.createUserWithCredentials]. Tags: user. Create user with credentials. Create a new user with email and password and add them to the active organization with the specified role. Only available in self-hosted mode.. Parameters: email (required), password (required), role (required)

user-sendInvitation [POST /user.sendInvitation]. Tags: user. Send invitation email. Send an invitation email to a pending invitee using a configured email or Resend notification provider. Returns the generated invite link. Disabled on cloud.. Parameters: invitationId (required), notificationId (required)
user-getBookmarkedTemplates [GET /user.getBookmarkedTemplates]. Tags: user. Get bookmarked templates. Retrieve the list of template IDs that the current user has bookmarked.

user-toggleTemplateBookmark [POST /user.toggleTemplateBookmark]. Tags: user. Toggle template bookmark. Add or remove a template from the current user's bookmarks. Returns whether the template is now bookmarked.. Parameters: templateId (required)
ai-one [GET /ai.one]. Tags: ai. Get AI settings by ID. Returns a single AI provider configuration by its ID.. Parameters: aiId (required)

ai-getModels [GET /ai.getModels]. Tags: ai. List available AI models. Fetches the list of models from the given AI provider URL. Supports OpenAI-compatible, Ollama, Gemini, Perplexity, ZAI, and MiniMax providers.. Parameters: apiUrl (required), apiKey (required)

ai-create [POST /ai.create]. Tags: ai. Create AI provider. Saves a new AI provider configuration (API URL, key, model) for the current organization.. Parameters: name (required), apiUrl (required), apiKey (required), model (required), isEnabled (required)

ai-update [POST /ai.update]. Tags: ai. Update AI provider. Updates an existing AI provider configuration for the current organization.. Parameters: aiId (required), name (optional), apiUrl (optional), apiKey (optional), model (optional), isEnabled (optional), createdAt (optional)
ai-getAll [GET /ai.getAll]. Tags: ai. List all AI providers. Returns all AI provider configurations for the current organization.
ai-get [GET /ai.get]. Tags: ai. Get AI provider. Returns a single AI provider configuration by its ID.. Parameters: aiId (required)
ai-delete [POST /ai.delete]. Tags: ai. Delete AI provider. Removes an AI provider configuration by its ID.. Parameters: aiId (required)

ai-getEnabledProviders [GET /ai.getEnabledProviders]. Tags: ai. List enabled AI providers. Returns a lightweight list of enabled AI providers (ID, name, model) for the current organization, suitable for dropdown selectors.

ai-analyzeLogs [POST /ai.analyzeLogs]. Tags: ai. Analyze logs with AI. Sends build or runtime logs to the specified AI provider for analysis. Returns a summary of issues found, root causes, and suggested fixes.. Parameters: aiId (required), logs (required), context (required) [build|runtime]

ai-testConnection [POST /ai.testConnection]. Tags: ai. Test AI provider connection. Sends a minimal prompt to the specified AI provider and model to verify the API URL, key, and model are valid and reachable.. Parameters: apiUrl (required), apiKey (required), model (required)

ai-suggest [POST /ai.suggest]. Tags: ai. Suggest deployment variants. Uses AI to generate deployment configuration suggestions (docker-compose variants) based on the user's input prompt.. Parameters: aiId (required), input (required), serverId (optional)

ai-deploy [POST /ai.deploy]. Tags: ai. Deploy AI suggestion. Deploys an AI-generated suggestion by creating a compose service with its docker-compose file, environment variables, domains, and config file mounts.. Parameters: environmentId (required), id (required), dockerCompose (required), envVariables (required), serverId (optional), name (required), description (required), domains (optional), configFiles (optional)

organization-all [GET /organization.all]. Tags: organization. List all organizations. Retrieve all organizations the current user is a member of, including their membership details.

organization-one [GET /organization.one]. Tags: organization. Get an organization by ID. Retrieve a single organization by its ID. The current user must be a member of the organization.. Parameters: organizationId (required)

organization-update [POST /organization.update]. Tags: organization. Update an organization. Update the name and logo of an organization. Only the organization owner can perform this action.. Parameters: organizationId (required), name (required), logo (optional)

organization-delete [POST /organization.delete]. Tags: organization. Delete an organization. Delete an organization by ID. Only the owner can delete it, and they must retain at least one organization.. Parameters: organizationId (required)

organization-inviteMember [POST /organization.inviteMember]. Tags: organization. Invite a member to organization. Parameters: email (required), role (required)

organization-allInvitations [GET /organization.allInvitations]. Tags: organization. List all organization invitations. Retrieve all invitations for the active organization, ordered by status and expiration date.

organization-removeInvitation [POST /organization.removeInvitation]. Tags: organization. Remove an invitation. Delete a pending invitation by ID. Only invitations belonging to the active organization can be removed.. Parameters: invitationId (required)

organization-updateMemberRole [POST /organization.updateMemberRole]. Tags: organization. Update member role. Change the role of a member in the active organization. organization-setDefault [POST /organization.setDefault]. Tags: organization. Set default organization. Set an organization as the default for the current user. Unsets any previous default and marks the specified organization as the new default.. Parameters: organizationId (required)

organization-active [GET /organization.active]. Tags: organization. Get active organization. Retrieve the organization that is currently active in the user's session. Returns null if no organization is active.

volumeBackups-delete [POST /volumeBackups.delete]. Tags: volumeBackups. Delete a volume backup. Permanently removes a volume backup configuration by its ID.. Parameters: volumeBackupId (required)

volumeBackups-update [POST /volumeBackups.update]. Tags: volumeBackups. Update a volume backup. Updates an existing volume backup configuration. Checks creation permissions and logs an audit event.. Parameters: name (required), description (optional), projectId (required)

environment-one [GET /environment.one]. Tags: environment. Get environment. Returns a single environment by ID with all its services. Non-admin users only see services they have been granted access to.. Parameters: environmentId (required)

environment-remove [POST /environment.remove]. Tags: environment. Delete environment. Deletes an environment by ID. The default environment cannot be deleted. Checks deletion permissions and environment access before removing.. Parameters: environmentId (required)

environment-update [POST /environment.update]. Tags: environment. Update environment. Updates an environment's name, description, or env variables. The default environment cannot be renamed. Checks environment access and env-var write permissions.. Parameters: environmentId (required), name (optional), description (optional), projectId (optional), env (optional) — environment variables as a single string, one per line in KEY=VALUE format

environment-duplicate [POST /environment.duplicate]. Tags: environment. Duplicate environment. Creates a copy of an existing environment including its services. Checks environment access and organization ownership before duplicating.. Parameters: environmentId (required), name (required), description (optional)

environment-search [GET /environment.search]. Tags: environment. Search environments. Searches environments by name, description, or project with pagination. Non-admin users only see environments they have been granted access to.. Parameters: q (optional), name (optional), description (optional), projectId (optional), limit (optional), offset (optional)

tag-create [POST /tag.create]. Tags: tag. Create tag. Creates a new tag with a name and color for the current organization. Tag names must be unique within the organization.. Parameters: name (required), color (optional)
tag-all [GET /tag.all]. Tags: tag. List all tags. Returns all tags for the current organization, ordered alphabetically by name.
tag-one [GET /tag.one]. Tags: tag. Get tag. Returns a single tag by ID. Only returns tags belonging to the caller's organization.. Parameters: tagId (required)

tag-update [POST /tag.update]. Tags: tag. Update tag. Updates an existing tag's name and/or color. Verifies the tag belongs to the caller's organization. Tag names must remain unique.. Parameters: tagId (required), name (optional), color (optional), createdAt (optional), organizationId (optional)

tag-remove [POST /tag.remove]. Tags: tag. Delete tag. Deletes a tag by ID. Cascade-deletes all project-tag associations. Verifies the tag belongs to the caller's organization.. Parameters: tagId (required)

tag-removeFromProject [POST /tag.removeFromProject]. Tags: tag. Remove tag from project. Removes a tag-project association. Verifies that both the tag and project belong to the caller's organization and that the caller has project access.. Parameters: projectId (required), tagId (required)

patch-create [POST /patch.create]. Tags: patch. Create patch. Creates a new file patch for an application or compose service. Checks service-level permissions and logs an audit event.. Parameters: filePath (required) — path to a specific file in the repository, e.g. 'docker-compose.yml' or 'src/index.ts', content (required), type (optional) [create|update|delete], enabled (optional), applicationId (optional), composeId (optional)
patch-one [GET /patch.one]. Tags: patch. Get patch. Returns a single patch by ID. Resolves the associated service to verify read permissions.. Parameters: patchId (required)
patch-byEntityId [GET /patch.byEntityId]. Tags: patch. List patches by entity. Returns all patches associated with a given application or compose service.. Parameters: id (required), type (required)

patch-update [POST /patch.update]. Tags: patch. Update patch. Updates the content or configuration of an existing patch. Resolves the associated service to verify permissions and logs an audit event.. Parameters: patchId (required), type (optional) [create|update|delete], filePath (optional) — path to a specific file in the repository, e.g. 'docker-compose.yml' or 'src/index.ts', enabled (optional), content (optional), createdAt (optional), updatedAt (optional)

patch-delete [POST /patch.delete]. Tags: patch. Delete patch. Deletes a patch by ID. Resolves the associated service to verify delete permissions and logs an audit event.. Parameters: patchId (required)

patch-toggleEnabled [POST /patch.toggleEnabled]. Tags: patch. Toggle patch enabled state. Enables or disables a patch without deleting it. Resolves the associated service to verify permissions and logs an audit event.. Parameters: patchId (required), enabled (required)

patch-ensureRepo [POST /patch.ensureRepo]. Tags: patch. Ensure patch repository exists. Ensures a patch repository is initialized for the given application or compose service. Creates the repo if it does not exist and logs an audit event.. Parameters: id (required), type (required) [application|compose]

patch-readRepoDirectories [GET /patch.readRepoDirectories]. Tags: patch. List patch repository directories. Reads the directory listing at a given path inside the patch repository for an application or compose service.. Parameters: id (required), type (required), repoPath (required)

patch-readRepoFile [GET /patch.readRepoFile]. Tags: patch. Read patch repository file. Reads a file from the patch repository. Parameters: id (required), type (required), filePath (required)

patch-saveFileAsPatch [POST /patch.saveFileAsPatch]. Tags: patch. Save file as patch. Creates or updates a patch record from file content. If a patch already exists for the file path, it updates the existing patch; otherwise creates a new one.. Parameters: id (required), type (required) [application|compose], filePath (required) — path to a specific file in the repository, e.g. 'docker-compose.yml' or 'src/index.ts', content (required), patchType (optional) [create|update]

patch-markFileForDeletion [POST /patch.markFileForDeletion]. Tags: patch. Mark file for deletion. Creates a delete-type patch that will remove the specified file from the service on next deployment. Logs an audit event.. Parameters: id (required), type (required) [application|compose], filePath (required) — path to a specific file in the repository, e.g. 'docker-compose.yml' or 'src/index.ts'

patch-cleanPatchRepos [POST /patch.cleanPatchRepos]. Tags: patch. Clean patch repositories. Removes all patch repository working directories on the local or a specified remote server. Admin-only operation that logs an audit event.. Parameters: serverId (optional)
